title: cocculus carolinus
intro: Cocculus carolinus, also known as the King's cancer or Coralbead, is a small, woody climber plant native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cocculus carolinus include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of extracts, tinctures, or dried roots, which can be taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
